name: plan_manifest_wave2_data_plane
description: Teach the deterministic data plane to load and persist plan/approval manifests by registry id before falling back to files.

jobs.label: wire_data_plane_to_control_manifest_registry
jobs.prompt:
## Goal
Make the deterministic data plane registry-first for `reconcile`, `approve`, and `apply` so plan/approval state becomes durable and surface-agnostic.

## Read first
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/artifacts/workflow/_control_manifest_registry_contract.json`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_plane_manifests.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/contracts/data_contracts.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_plane.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/core/data_ops.py`
- `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_data_plane.py`

## Required changes
1. Update `contracts/data_contracts.py` to accept `plan_manifest_id` and `approval_manifest_id` as first-class inputs.
2. Update `runtime/data_plane.py` so `_load_plan(...)` and `_load_approval(...)` resolve manifest ids through the control-plane manifest owner when a Postgres connection is available.
3. Keep inline payloads and repo-local JSON paths as fallback import/export lanes.
4. On `reconcile`, persist a draft plan manifest when Postgres is available and expose the manifest id in the receipt.
5. On `approve`, persist an approval manifest and advance the linked plan manifest to `approved` when Postgres is available.
6. On `apply`, support loading both plan and approval by manifest id and advance the plan manifest to `applied` after a successful apply.
7. Include machine-readable manifest references in the receipt payload.

## Constraints
- Do not make Postgres mandatory for ordinary data-plane execution.
- If a manifest id is provided and Postgres is unavailable, fail closed with a structured boundary/runtime error.
- Do not remove the current workspace-boundary checks.

## Required tests
Update `Code&DBs/Workflow/tests/unit/test_data_plane.py` to cover:
- normalize accepts `plan_manifest_id` / `approval_manifest_id`
- reconcile persists a plan manifest when a fake pg connection is passed
- approve can load a plan via manifest id and emits an approval manifest ref
- apply can load plan/approval via manifest ids
- file-path fallback still works

Database: `postgresql://nate@127.0.0.1:5432/dag_workflow`
